purposes.description: Convert 15-minute USGS streamflow readings to daily maximum discharge values using pandas resampling.

primitives.evidence: Requires pandas for CSV parsing with parse_dates, set_index, and resample('D').max().
Must write and execute Python script to process CSV data.

purposes.description: Classify flood events by NWS severity levels (action, minor, moderate, major) and count flood days above the action threshold.

primitives.evidence: Standard library-level logic: nested if/elif comparisons against threshold dictionary values.
Simple if-then deduction: compare peak flow against ordered thresholds to determine severity category.
Flood day counting must use the action threshold specifically, not minor/moderate/major.

purposes.description: Estimate flood return periods (10-yr, 50-yr, 100-yr) using the Log-Pearson Type III distribution with frequency factors.

primitives.evidence: Requires numpy (np.log10, np.mean, np.std) and scipy.stats.skew for LP3 statistical analysis.
Multi-step: log-transform peaks, compute sample mean/std/skew, look up K factors, compute quantile as 10^(mean + K*std).
Must use sample std (ddof=1) not population std, sample skewness (bias=False), and one peak per year.
Must write and execute Python script for numerical computation.
